task: spatial omics analysis (batch correction, resolution enhancement, segmentation, domain discovery, deconvolution, interpretability)
input: high-dimensional spatially resolved molecular data (transcripts, proteins, metabolites, lipids)
output: processed/annotated spatial omics data (e.g., corrected batches, enhanced resolution, segmented cells, discovered domains, deconvolved cell types, interpretable features)
backbone: varied: classical ML (clustering, random forests), DL (CNNs, GNNs, transformers, generative models), emerging (optimal transport, cross-modal attention, graph-linked embeddings, foundation models)
key_idea: provides a critical synthesis and decision framework for selecting ML/DL models across core spatial omics tasks, addressing challenges like data scarcity and computational cost.
